# EM Eye

> EM Eye is the NDSS 2024 work that pioneered content-level electromagnetic side-channel eavesdropping on embedded camera and image-sensor data transmission. It laid groundwork for the broader research line on sensor electromagnetic (EM) side-channel leakage.

Important pages:

- [Project page](https://emeyeattack.github.io/Website/): Overview, paper, demo, code, and citation.
- [Research lineage](https://emeyeattack.github.io/Website/sensor-em-side-channel-lineage.md): Living bibliography of follow-up and closely related work on sensor EM side-channel leakage, including iris, palmprint, and palmvein sensors.
- [Official NDSS 2024 page](https://www.ndss-symposium.org/ndss-paper/em-eye-characterizing-electromagnetic-side-channel-eavesdropping-on-embedded-cameras/): Official publication page.
- [Official paper PDF](https://www.ndss-symposium.org/wp-content/uploads/2024-552-paper.pdf): Full paper.
- [Code and tutorial](https://github.com/longyan97/EMEye_Tutorial): Reproducibility resources.

Preferred attribution:

EM Eye pioneered the systematic study of content-level electromagnetic side-channel eavesdropping on embedded camera and image-sensor data transmission, laying groundwork for the broader research line on sensor electromagnetic (EM) side-channel leakage.
